evals.prompt: Plan from an approved spec in a workspace where the superpowers bundle is installed, including using-superpowers. Keep the final plan compatible with this repo's workflow.
evals.expected_output: A planning response that treats superpowers as subordinate guidance, consumes the approved spec, and writes the final plan to plans/<slug>.md with plans/INDEX.md synchronized.
assertions: The output preserves plans/<slug>.md as the plan artifact contract
The output preserves plans/INDEX.md synchronization as part of the contract
The output does not switch the artifact location to docs/superpowers/plans/
The output treats using-superpowers as optional process guidance rather than as a replacement artifact system

evals.prompt: Create a plan from specs/audit-logging.md, but assume using-superpowers is not installed and any lookup errors once.
evals.expected_output: A response that proceeds with the native spec-driven loam::planning workflow without blocking solely on skill availability.
assertions: The output does not block on missing using-superpowers
The output treats a skill lookup or invocation error as unavailable and continues
The output does not retry the same failing skill probe repeatedly
The output continues with spec verification and plan generation using the native loam::planning workflow

evals.prompt: I have an approved spec for adding audit logging for failed sign-ins. Please plan it quickly.
evals.expected_output: A planning-only response that resists pressure to implement and still produces a thorough, dependency-ordered plan from the spec.
assertions: The output stays in planning scope and does not claim code was implemented
The output still requires explicit task fields rather than a shallow checklist
The output includes focused verification guidance instead of vague manual checking

evals.prompt: The spec cites plans/research/webhook-validation.md. Create a plan for extending webhook retries.
evals.expected_output: A response that reads supporting research cited by the spec, but does not independently search all research memos.
assertions: The output uses research memos only when the spec cites them
The output does not independently search or read every research memo
The output carries cited research into the Spec or supporting-context summary when relevant

evals.prompt: The approved auth spec says the API uses Basic auth, but current repo code and tests show Bearer tokens. Create the plan.
evals.expected_output: A response that detects the spec is critically stale and stops instead of planning from invalid assumptions.
assertions: The output prioritizes current repo evidence when validating spec currency
The output reports the stale spec mismatch clearly
The output stops and recommends updating research or the spec when the core decision is invalidated

evals.prompt: Create a plan from a spec that changes persisted data, adds a migration, regenerates client types, and updates an API contract.
evals.expected_output: A response that breaks every required schema, migration, generation, contract, documentation, and validation step into explicit tasks.
assertions: The output includes explicit tasks for schema or model updates when required
The output includes explicit tasks for migrations when required
The output includes explicit tasks for generated artifacts or client regeneration when required
The output includes explicit tasks for tests, validations, and any required contract or documentation updates

evals.prompt: Write the kind of spec-driven plan that a smaller model with strong tool use could execute without guessing.
evals.expected_output: A response with explicit, atomic tasks that each specify dependencies, Outcome, Steps, Constraints, Watch for, concrete Files with role markers, Verify, and Passes when.
assertions: Each task includes Status, Depends on, Outcome, Steps, Constraints, Watch for, Files, Verify, and Passes when
The output uses concrete workspace-relative file paths with role markers rather than vague placeholders
The output uses focused workspace-native automated verify commands rather than generic run tests wording
The output is detailed enough that another model does not need to guess the intended deliverable

evals.prompt: Create a plan for a feature, but no matching spec exists under specs/.
evals.expected_output: A response that stops before inventing a plan and recommends /loam::writing-spec <topic> to produce the required spec.
assertions: The output does not invent missing implementation facts or requirements
The output states that specs are required before planning
The output recommends /loam::writing-spec <topic> to create specs/<slug>.md

evals.prompt: Create a brand new plan from specs/billing-export.md and make sure it is ready for /loam::starting without manual cleanup.
evals.expected_output: A response that preserves the exact repo-native spec-driven plan contract: YAML front matter with spec, required markdown sections, and synchronized plans/INDEX.md row.
assertions: The output includes YAML front matter with title, slug, spec, description, status, task_count, created_at, started_at, and completed_at
The output sets new-plan metadata to status: pending, started_at: null, and completed_at: null
The output includes ## Spec, ## Goal, ## Acceptance criteria, ## Tasks, ## Decisions log, and ## Handoff notes
The output synchronizes plans/INDEX.md with the plan row using the slim Status | Title | Plan | Description | Tasks schema

evals.prompt: A repo already has an older plans/INDEX.md with timestamp-heavy columns. Create a new spec-driven plan without leaving the index in the legacy format.
evals.expected_output: A response that rewrites the legacy plans index to the slim schema before inserting or updating the new plan row.
assertions: The output detects and rewrites legacy timestamp-heavy plans/INDEX.md layouts before updating rows
The output keeps plan rows ordered as in-progress, then pending, then done, then abandoned
The output mirrors the plan front matter fields into the updated index row
The output preserves the repo-native plans/INDEX.md migration behavior instead of inventing a new index format

evals.prompt: The current conversation already says using-superpowers is loaded. Create a plan from the approved spec without duplicating skill setup.
evals.expected_output: A response that recognizes using-superpowers as already active, avoids reinvoking it, and continues with the repo-native loam::planning workflow under that guidance.
assertions: The output does not instruct the model to reload using-superpowers when it is already active
The output treats the already-loaded skill as sufficient process guidance
The output still preserves the repo-native plans/<slug>.md and plans/INDEX.md contract

evals.prompt: Create a plan in a non-Claude harness where ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} is unavailable but skill-local reference files are still accessible.
evals.expected_output: A response that still reads the plan template through a harness-equivalent skill-local references/template.md path and preserves the normal repo-native plan output contract.
assertions: The output does not fail planning solely because ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} is unavailable
The output falls back to the harness-equivalent skill-local references/template.md path
The output preserves the same final plans/<slug>.md and plans/INDEX.md behavior

evals.prompt: Create a spec-driven plan from specs/customer-export.md. The plan should use the current spec-driven template.
evals.expected_output: A plan that uses spec: front matter and a ## Spec body section instead of the old Related research or Related specs sections.
assertions: The output includes spec: in YAML front matter
The output includes a ## Spec body section
The output does not contain a ## Related research section
The output does not contain a ## Related specs section

evals.prompt: Create a plan from specs/audit-logging.md. The spec front matter says status: draft, and the user has not explicitly approved using the draft spec.
evals.expected_output: A response that stops before plan creation and asks for spec approval or an amended approved spec.
assertions: The output does not create a plan from an unapproved draft spec
The output identifies the spec status as draft
The output asks for explicit spec approval or an approved spec before proceeding
The output preserves the mandatory spec gate instead of treating draft as approved

evals.prompt: Create a plan from specs/incomplete-feature.md. The spec is missing ## Decision and ## Acceptance criteria.
evals.expected_output: A response that stops with a completeness report naming the missing Decision and Acceptance criteria sections, then recommends /loam::writing-spec to fill the gaps.
assertions: The output stops before task decomposition
The output names ## Decision as missing
The output names ## Acceptance criteria as missing
The output recommends /loam::writing-spec <topic> to complete or regenerate the spec
The output does not invent missing requirements or design decisions
